description:
You are helping a developer clean-up an optimization project that lives in
    /home/user/opt_project.  
    The project already contains several linear-programming model files
    (extensions “.lp” and “.mps”) scattered in different sub-directories, as
    well as many source and data files that must remain untouched.

    Your job is to:

    1.  Create a compressed tarball named
        /home/user/opt_project/models_bundle.tar.gz that contains **all** files
        whose names end exactly in “.lp” or “.mps” that are located anywhere
        inside /home/user/opt_project when you start the task.  
        •  Inside the archive the paths must remain **relative to
           /home/user/opt_project**, preserving every sub-directory structure.  
        •  No other file types may appear in the archive.

    2.  Immediately after creating the archive, compute its SHA-256 checksum and
        write a one-line log file called
        /home/user/opt_project/models_bundle.log.

        The line must be in this exact format (note the *two* spaces between the
        hash and the path, and the final line-feed):

            <64-hex-digit-sha256>␠␠/home/user/opt_project/models_bundle.tar.gz\n

        Example (the hash below is **only** an illustration):

            8e7c3a7e4a445ab3f4b1b97c5c8b8d4cd2ab4adab0e65f0c16c4833a7c8a9c61  /home/user/opt_project/models_bundle.tar.gz

        •  There must be **exactly one** line in the file.
        •  The SHA-256 you write must match the archive that currently exists on
           disk, so calculate it *after* creating the tarball.

    3.  Solve the task using **one single compound terminal command** (you may
        use pipes, subshells, command substitutions, logical operators, etc., as
        long as everything is issued as a single line at the prompt).

    Verification notes for the automated test:
        •  The test will untar models_bundle.tar.gz and confirm that it contains
           every “.lp” and “.mps” file that existed under
           /home/user/opt_project at task start, and contains nothing else.  
        •  It will read models_bundle.log and verify that:
              1. The file contains exactly one line.
              2. The 64-character hexadecimal string matches the SHA-256 digest
                 of models_bundle.tar.gz.
              3. After two consecutive space characters, the absolute path in
                 the log is exactly: /home/user/opt_project/models_bundle.tar.gz

    Provide no additional commentary or explanation in the terminal; only run
    your one-line command to accomplish the task.
truth:
Initial file layout (all paths are absolute):
        /home/user/opt_project/README.md
        /home/user/opt_project/setup.py
        /home/user/opt_project/src/solver.py
        /home/user/opt_project/src/models/modelA.lp
        /home/user/opt_project/src/models/modelB.mps
        /home/user/opt_project/data/demand.csv
        /home/user/opt_project/data/scenario.lp
        /home/user/opt_project/tests/test_parser.py
        /home/user/opt_project/legacy/old_model.mps

    Summary:
        Optimization model files present at start (must be inside archive):
            src/models/modelA.lp
            src/models/modelB.mps
            data/scenario.lp
            legacy/old_model.mps

        Non-model files present at start (must NOT be in archive):
            README.md
            setup.py
            src/solver.py
            data/demand.csv
            tests/test_parser.py

    After successful execution the following **must** exist:
        1. /home/user/opt_project/models_bundle.tar.gz
              •  The tarball contains exactly the four relative paths listed
                 above and nothing else.
        2. /home/user/opt_project/models_bundle.log
              •  One text line: “<sha256>␠␠/home/user/opt_project/models_bundle.tar.gz\n”
              •  The sha256 string is the digest of models_bundle.tar.gz.

    The automated checker will:
        •  Expand the tar archive, compare its file list against the set
          {src/models/modelA.lp, src/models/modelB.mps,
           data/scenario.lp, legacy/old_model.mps}.
        •  Read models_bundle.log, split on two consecutive spaces, verify
          SHA-256 matches tarball.  No extra lines allowed.
